questions.question: How does the application authenticate users and manage their identity lifecycle? (SSO + MFA + IGA)

elements.text: Are there bypass paths to SSO still in place (e.g. direct URLs or local accounts permitted)?
Is MFA only enforced on some access paths but not all?
Is identity lifecycle management manual, ad-hoc, or lacking a quarterly review process?
Has the application been fully onboarded to Ping SSO with no alternate login paths permitted?
Is MFA enforced for all interactive access, including users, admins, and AWS console / CLI / API?
Is identity lifecycle managed centrally via MyAccess (SailPoint IGA) with quarterly access reviews?
Is the session inactivity timeout set to 15 minutes or less?
Is Zscaler device restriction active and enforced?
Are breakglass procedures documented with a defined activation process?
Is automated provisioning and deprovisioning in place via IGA workflows, with no manual ticket-driven onboarding?
Is access anomaly detection actively running and generating alerts?
Is a service account inventory maintained with credential rotation tracking?
Is RBAC fully defined and mapped to business roles?
Do all service accounts use short-lived credentials (STS / OIDC tokens) with no static keys in use?
Are zero standing privileges enforced, with admin access granted on a JIT basis only?
Is access certification performed on a continuous basis, not limited to quarterly reviews?
Is Identity Threat Detection and Response (ITDR) deployed and operational?

questions.question: Are privileged + service accounts in CyberArk with automated rotation, and breakglass accounts properly stored?

elements.text: Are any privileged accounts existing outside of CyberArk?
Is credential rotation manual or infrequent?
Is the breakglass procedure undocumented or shared too broadly?
Are all privileged and service accounts integrated with CyberArk PAM with automated credential rotation?
Is JIT access enforced for all human admins?
Are breakglass accounts stored in CyberArk or a physical safe with a documented activation procedure?
Is PAM coverage reconciled daily against the ServiceNow privileged-account inventory with drift alerts active?
Is session recording enabled on all privileged sessions?
Is just-in-time elevation in place for routine admin tasks, with no long-running admin shells permitted?
Do all privileged sessions route through Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs)?
Are privileged credentials never exposed outside the vault, with all access via proxy-based methods?
Is behavioural analytics active and monitoring privileged activity?

questions.question: Is least-privilege enforced (verified by Wiz / IAM Access Analyzer) and AD/PARIS integration in place?

elements.text: Are IAM policies coarse-grained (e.g. broad admin or power-user roles) with no automated check for over-permissive policies?
Is AD/PARIS integration ad-hoc or incomplete?
Are IAM roles and policies verified as least-privilege via Wiz CSPM or IAM Access Analyzer with no over-permissive policies?
Is the application integrated with enterprise AD for authorisation, with PARIS registration for infrastructure access?
Are IAM findings tracked to SLA-based remediation with no overdue critical or high findings?
Are permission boundaries enforced on all IAM roles?
Is cross-account access reviewed on a regular basis?
Are just-in-time policy adjustments made based on usage telemetry?
Is policy-as-code in place with automated policy linting integrated in the CI pipeline?
Is policy drift detected and auto-remediated?

questions.question: Are secrets vaulted and sessions hardened? (no plaintext creds; 15-min idle / 24-hr absolute timeout)

elements.text: Are any secrets stored as plaintext in source code, config files, or environment variables?
Are session timeouts inconsistent, absent, or not server-side enforced?
Is automated secret scanning absent?
Are all application secrets (API keys, DB credentials, tokens) stored in an approved vault (CyberArk / AWS Secrets Manager) with no plaintext credentials in source or config?
Are user sessions configured with a 15-minute inactivity timeout and a 24-hour absolute timeout, enforced server-side?
Is GitHub Enterprise Secret Scanning and Wiz secret detection running across all repos and config?
Are secret scanning findings tracked to a remediation SLA?
Are secret rotation policies enforced across all secrets?
Are secrets injected at runtime via short-lived dynamic credentials, with no secrets resident in application config?
Are sessions cryptographically signed and bound to the device?

questions.question: Are quarterly access reviews completed and default/temp accounts removed?

elements.text: Are access reviews ad-hoc or only triggered by audit findings rather than on a scheduled basis?
Are default or temporary accounts from implementation still active or not actively pruned?
Are quarterly access reviews performed covering least-privilege validation, orphaned accounts, and role appropriateness, with action items closed within 30 days?
Have all temporary and default native accounts created during implementation been removed or disabled?
Does review tooling automatically pre-populate reviewers with usage telemetry such as time-since-last-use and sensitive-resource access patterns?
Is orphaned account detection running on a daily basis?
Are ML-assisted reviewer recommendations in place to flag anomalous entitlements compared to peers?
Are micro-attestations triggered automatically on role changes, not just on a calendar cadence?

questions.question: Is data encrypted at rest (KMS, rotated), in transit (TLS 1.2+), aligned to GIC crypto standards, and SOC2/OSPAR vendor assurance current?

elements.text: Is encryption at rest only partially enabled, with some services or data stores unprotected?
Are there any internal hops where TLS 1.2+ is not enforced?
Are SOC2/OSPAR vendor attestations outdated or missing for third-party vendors handling sensitive data?
Is all sensitive data encrypted at rest using KMS-managed keys with automated rotation within policy limits?
Is TLS 1.2+ enforced for all data in transit with no unencrypted internal hops?
Are GIC mandatory cryptography controls adhered to for algorithms and protocols?
Have SOC2 Type 2 / OSPAR / ISO 27001 attestations been obtained and reviewed within the last 12 months for all third-party vendors handling sensitive data?
Is automated data discovery and classification running continuously?
Is key rotation performed within 90 days?
Is data lineage tracked across the estate?
Is tokenisation applied to sensitive data fields?
Is production data prohibited from non-production environments without explicit approval and masking?
Is envelope encryption with per-tenant keys implemented?
Is automated DLP with ML-based data classification in place?
Is crypto agility in place, allowing algorithm rotation without downtime?
Is hardware-backed key storage implemented?

questions.question: Is data classified per GIC scheme and Secret data restricted to dedicated Singapore infrastructure?

elements.text: Is data classification only partially applied or existing only on paper?
Is any Secret-classified data hosted on shared infrastructure or outside Singapore?
Is all data classified per the GIC scheme (Public / Internal / Restricted / Secret)?
Is Secret-classified information stored exclusively on dedicated infrastructure in GIC-managed Singapore data centres with restricted access?
Are automated data discovery and classification scans running continuously?
Do classification labels propagate through data lineage tracking?
Is production data prohibited from landing in non-production environments without approval and masking?
Is per-tenant isolation implemented for Secret data using envelope encryption?
Is automated re-classification triggered on data drift?
Is data residency enforced via policy-as-code, preventing workloads from deploying outside approved regions?

questions.question: Is non-prod data masked, and are dev/UAT segregated from prod?

elements.text: Is production data being refreshed unmasked into non-production environments?
Are dev and UAT environments insufficiently segregated from production?
Are debug tools or compilers present in the production environment?
Is PII and sensitive data masked or anonymised when refreshed to non-production environments?
Is production data retained in non-production environments for no more than 30 days?
Are dev and UAT environments segregated from production, with no compilers, dev tools, or debug utilities in prod?
Are synthetic data generation pipelines in place for non-production testing?
Is automated detection of unmasked production data in lower environments active?
Does data lineage tracking confirm masking is applied at every refresh?
Is per-tenant tokenisation applied in non-production environments?
Is automated lifecycle management of test data in place, including auto-purge after each sprint?
Is referential integrity preserved across all masked datasets?

questions.question: Are DLP / egress controls in place (Zscaler, S3 policies, monitored)?
questions.industry_source: CIS 13.3; OWASP A04; AWS WAF Security

elements.text: Is egress traffic largely unrestricted with no effective DLP policies enforced?
Are any S3 buckets publicly accessible?
Is Zscaler SSL inspection only partially applied?
Are data exfiltration controls enforced, including egress filtering, DLP policies, and S3 bucket policies?
Is all internet traffic routed through Zscaler SSL inspection?
Is monitoring in place for anomalous egress activity?
Is DLP alert volume baselined and reviewed monthly?
Are S3 bucket policies reviewed on a quarterly basis?
Is egress anomaly detection (by volume and destination) wired to the SOC?
Is ML-based DLP classification in place, going beyond keyword matching?
Is zero-trust egress enforced with a deny-by-default policy and explicit allow-lists?
Is CASB integrated for visibility and control over SaaS data flows?

questions.question: Are CodeQL + Snyk + Secret Scanning integrated in CI/CD with critical/high blocking promotion?
questions.industry_source: CIS 16.11; OWASP A06; NIST SSDF PW.6, PW.7

elements.text: Are security scanning findings failing to block promotion to production?
Is secret scanning only partial with no SLA on remediation?
Are CodeQL (SAST), Snyk (SCA), and GitHub Enterprise Secret Scanning integrated in CI/CD with critical and high findings blocking promotion to production?
Are all vulnerability findings tracked to SLA-based remediation?
Are vulnerability SLA metrics tracked and reported?
Is DAST integrated in the staging environment?
Is container image scanning performed before every deployment?
Is a WAF deployed for all internet-exposed endpoints?
Is auto-remediation in place for known vulnerability patterns?
Is Runtime Application Self-Protection (RASP) deployed?
Is a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) generated per release?
Is autonomous patching in place for non-breaking updates?
Is threat-intelligence-driven prioritisation used for vulnerability remediation?

questions.question: Is Tenable installed everywhere with monthly VAR, and is patch mgmt documented and on-SLA?

elements.text: Is the Tenable Agent missing from any server assets?
Is monthly VAR coverage incomplete?
Is the patch management process manual or untested?
Is the Tenable Agent installed on all server assets with monthly Vulnerability Assessment Report (VAR) coverage?
Is a documented patch management process in place covering automated deployment via GIC tools, lower-environment testing, and SLA-based remediation?
Are patch compliance metrics tracked and reported monthly?
Are container base images automatically rebuilt when new CVEs are published?
Are overdue vulnerability findings dashboarded and visible to the team?
Is autonomous patching in place for non-breaking updates?
Is CVE-to-deploy time under 7 days?
Is ephemeral or immutable infrastructure used, eliminating the need for in-place patching?

questions.question: Pen-test completed with crit/high closed, Wiz CSPM clean, and conditional AI/Mobile checklists done?

elements.text: Is the last penetration test more than 12 months old or has one never been conducted?
Are there outstanding critical findings in Wiz CSPM?
Have the AI and Mobile security checklists been skipped where applicable?
Has penetration testing been completed prior to go-live per TICRP criteria with all critical and high findings remediated?
Is Wiz CSPM running continuously with no outstanding critical misconfigurations?
Have the AI Security checklist (via ITRM) and Mobile Application Security checklist been completed where applicable?
Is penetration testing conducted quarterly for Tier-1 applications?
Are Wiz CSPM findings tracked to SLA-based remediation?
Is a threat model in place that is updated on architecture changes?
Is an active bug bounty or red-team programme in place?
Is chaos and abuse-case testing automated in the CI/CD pipeline?
Is a security-champion model actively practised within the team?

questions.question: Is input validation enforced and file upload hardened (size/type/content/AV-scanned/outside web root)?

elements.text: Is input validation only performed client-side with no server-side enforcement?
Do file uploads accept any size or type without restriction?
Are uploaded files stored within the web root or not scanned for malware?
Is input validation enforced server-side, including parameterised queries, output encoding, and anti-CSRF controls?
Are file uploads validated for size, file type, and content, with files stored outside the web root and scanned for malware?
Are WAF rules tuned to application-specific traffic patterns?
Are security headers (CSP, HSTS, etc.) applied on all responses?
Is file upload sandboxing in place for content inspection?
Is a positive security (allow-list) model enforced at the WAF?
Is inline file detonation in a sandbox performed before file delivery?
Is CSP report-uri integrated with the SOC for visibility on policy violations?

questions.question: Is main branch protected with required reviews + required CI checks + signed commits?

elements.text: Is direct push to the main branch permitted in any repository?
Are PR reviews optional or limited to a single reviewer for production code?
Are CI checks not required before merging to the main branch?
Are main and release branches protected with required PR reviews (at least 1 reviewer, at least 2 for production code)?
Are required status checks enforced, including build, tests, SAST, and SCA passing before merge?
Are signed commits and verified contributors enforced on protected branches?
Is code-owner-based review routing in place?
Are required reviewer expertise tags enforced?
Are merges blocked on stale reviews?
Is trunk-based development with feature flags in place?
Is AI-assisted code review covering security and style active?
Is deployment lead-time under 1 day?

questions.question: Is deployment fully automated with rollback/blue-green/canary tested and approvals in place?
questions.industry_source: DORA 4 keys; AWS WAF Operational Excellence; NIST SSDF PO.5

elements.text: Are there any manual steps required on production servers during deployment?
Has the rollback mechanism never been tested?
Are deployment change approvals ad-hoc rather than formally enforced?
Is deployment fully automated via GitHub Actions with no manual production-server steps?
Is a tested rollback, blue-green, or canary deployment mechanism in place?
Are deployment approvals enforced via the change control process?
Are DORA Four Keys (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, Change Failure Rate, MTTR) tracked?
Are feature flags in place for progressive rollout of changes?
Is automated rollback triggered on deployment failure?
Is infrastructure drift detection active?
Is trunk-based development with continuous deployment in place?
Are commits and deployment artefacts cryptographically signed?
Is supply-chain security implemented at SLSA Level 2 or above?

questions.question: Is infrastructure defined as code, version-controlled, and deployed consistently across environments?

elements.text: Is any infrastructure hand-built or deployed via click-ops rather than code?
Do environments drift from each other due to inconsistent deployment practices?
Is all infrastructure defined as code (Terraform / CDK) with the same artefact deployed across dev, UAT, and production?
Is environment-specific configuration kept out of the IaC code itself?
Is IaC stored in version control with required peer review?
Is drift detection running daily and alerting on environment divergence?
Is IaC modularised and reused across applications?
Is policy-as-code (e.g. OPA / Sentinel) blocking non-compliant infrastructure at the PR stage?
Are ephemeral environments provisioned per pull request?

questions.question: Is the app multi-AZ with tested failover, including data stores?

elements.text: Is the application running in a single Availability Zone with no automatic failover?
Do data stores lack redundancy or have untested redundancy?
Is the application deployed across 2 or more Availability Zones with tested automatic failover?
Are critical data stores configured for multi-AZ or cross-region redundancy (e.g. RDS Multi-AZ, S3 cross-region)?
Is an availability SLO defined, measured, and alerted on?
Is graceful degradation under partial failure tested and validated?
Has the HA architecture been validated against documented scenario tests?
Is chaos testing conducted on a schedule with documented results?
Is self-healing infrastructure in place?
Is multi-region active-active or warm standby deployed with automated failover under 5 minutes?

questions.question: Are SLOs defined for your Application?

elements.text: Are there no formal SLOs defined, with reliability measured ad-hoc or only by incident count?
Are SLIs and SLOs defined for user-facing endpoints, covering availability and latency?
Are Datadog SLO monitors alerting when thresholds approaching unhealthy?
Does error-budget exhaustion trigger a feature-freeze decision?
Is SLO posture reviewed quarterly with stakeholders?
Are composite SLOs defined across all dependencies?
Is predictive burn-rate alerting in place, alerting on trajectory rather than just current state?
Is SLO-driven autoscaling configured?

questions.question: Does the app degrade gracefully on dependency failure? (circuit breakers, fallbacks, tested)
questions.industry_source: Google SRE; AWS WAF Reliability; ISO 25010

elements.text: Do dependency failures cascade and bring down the entire application?
Are circuit breakers and fallback mechanisms absent?
Does the application implement graceful degradation for non-critical dependency failures, including circuit breakers, fallbacks, and timeouts?
Has graceful degradation been verified via chaos or dependency-failure testing in non-production?
Are chaos and dependency failure tests run on a schedule with documented results?
Is graceful degradation validated for every external integration?
Is chaos engineering practised in production behind feature flags?

questions.question: Are backups automated to required RPO, with annual restore test and measured RTO?
questions.industry_source: CIS 11.5; NIST 800-53 CP-9; AWS WAF Reliability

elements.text: Is backup coverage incomplete, or has a restore test not been performed in the last 24 months?
Are automated backups configured for all critical data stores, meeting the required RPO?
Has backup restore been tested at least annually with documented results and a measured RTO?
Are restore tests conducted semi-annually?
Are cross-region backup copies maintained for Tier-1 data?
Are immutable backups in place (e.g. using Vault Lock)?
Are restore tests automated and run on a continuous rolling basis?
Is RTO measured statistically at P50 and P95?

questions.question: Is the DR plan signed off, aligned to RTO/RPO, and tested within 12 months?

elements.text: Is the DR plan unsigned, untested, or more than 12 months out of date?
Is a documented DR plan in place, signed off, aligned to the declared RTO and RPO, and including a recovery runbook?
Has DR testing been completed within the last 12 months with results formally signed off?
Is DR tested semi-annually with documented results?
Are RTO and RPO targets validated against current business requirements?
Is the recovery runbook updated after every DR test?
Are chaos game days conducted to exercise DR scenarios?
Is multi-region active-active or warm standby deployed with automated failover under 5 minutes, eliminating manual runbook execution

questions.question: Is Datadog (metrics/synthetics) + Splunk (logs) integrated and active?

elements.text: Are logs incomplete or inconsistently shipping to Splunk or Datadog?
Is monitoring limited to basic uptime checks with no structured observability in place?
Is Datadog integrated for service monitoring, including URLs, endpoints, APIs, certificates, and synthetics?
Are all application and infrastructure logs onboarded to Splunk with at least hourly shipping and log sources documented?
Are the Four Golden Signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation) dashboarded?
Is distributed tracing with correlation IDs implemented?
Is SLO-based alerting with burn-rate monitoring in place?
Is log-based anomaly detection active?
Is a daily agent reconciliation report running?
Is synthetic monitoring in place for all critical user paths?
Is AIOps in place for anomaly detection and alert noise reduction?
Is automated runbook execution triggered on alerts?
Is predictive alerting in place to detect degradation before user impact occurs?

questions.question: Is privileged-activity security monitoring + log sanitisation in place?

elements.text: Is security monitoring only partially implemented for privileged activities?
Do audit logs contain credentials, PII, tokens, or session IDs?
Is security monitoring enabled for all privileged activities (admin logins, privilege escalation, config changes, data access) with alerting wired to the SOC?
Are audit logs sanitised to ensure no credentials, PII, tokens, or session IDs are written to log stores?
Is SOC alert routing tested on a quarterly basis?
Are correlation rules tuned to application-specific patterns?
Are insider-threat detection rules active and wired to the SOC?
Is UEBA (User and Entity Behaviour Analytics) baselining active per application?
Is ML-based anomaly correlation active across multiple data sources?

questions.question: Are expiry alerts (>3mo lead) and daily agent-coverage reconciliation operating?

elements.text: Are SSL certificate or licence expiries only caught reactively after they cause failures?
Is there no daily agent reconciliation process in place?
Are automated alerts configured for SSL certificate, licence, and support contract expiry with at least 3 months lead time?
Is daily agent reconciliation in place for Datadog, Tenable, Splunk, and endpoint agents, with missing-agent escalation?
Are certificate and licence renewal tickets auto-generated 90 or more days before expiry?
Are agent coverage gaps remediated within 24 hours?
Is predictive expiry detection in place, factoring in ticket queue length and vendor lead time?
Is agent health scored per asset?

questions.question: Are your assets and accounts fully inventoried — ServiceNow/Helix matches reality, all account types listed with owners?
questions.industry_source: CIS Control 1, CIS Control 5; ISO 27001 A.5.9

elements.text: Does the ServiceNow / Helix CMDB show more than 5% drift from the actual deployed infrastructure?
Is the account list incomplete, out of date, or missing ACM approval traces?
Does the asset inventory in ServiceNow / Helix accurately reflect all deployed infrastructure, including servers, databases, load balancers, and network components?
Does the account list cover all breakglass, service, privileged, and application accounts with named owners and ACM approval traces?
Is the inventory reconciled daily against AWS Config and Wiz, with drift above 5% triggering a ticket?
Is the account list version-controlled?
Is the inventory auto-populated from cloud APIs with no manual CMDB entry required?
Is orphaned account auto-detection in place?

questions.question: Is your architecture documentation AWG-approved and current? (system arch + network diagrams + firewall + proxy rules)

elements.text: Is the architecture diagram stale (more than 12 months old), unapproved, or are firewall and proxy rules undocumented?
Is there an AWG-approved system architecture document covering components, data flows, integration points, and network connectivity?
Are network architecture diagrams, firewall rules, and proxy rules documented and kept current?
Is architecture documentation version-controlled and reviewed annually?
Are all documentation changes tracked through a PR process?
Is documentation generated directly from code and config to prevent drift?
Are Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) maintained?

questions.question: Is the app operationally ready? (SOPs + vendor support + certificate management + UAT sign-off complete)

elements.text: Are any operational SOPs incomplete or missing coverage for the relevant ops teams?
Are vendor support contacts missing or out of date?
Is UAT sign-off from the business owner absent?
Are SOPs completed for all applicable ops teams (ServiceDesk, End User Infra, Compute, Cloud, IAM Ops)?
Are vendor support contacts documented, including 24x7 support and escalation paths?
Are certificate SOPs in place covering a maximum validity of 398 days and automated expiry monitoring?
Has UAT been formally signed off by the business owner?
Are operational metrics tracked, including MTTR, ticket volume, and change success rate?
Are post-incident reviews conducted with tracked action items?
Is a knowledge base actively maintained?
Are self-service operations in place, including ChatOps and automated remediation?
Is documentation generated from code and config rather than maintained manually?
Are operational excellence reviews conducted quarterly?

questions.question: Is auto-scale configured against measured baselines and tested at 1.5× peak?

elements.text: Is auto-scaling absent or untested, with no load test conducted in the last 12 months?
Are auto-scaling policies configured against measured baselines (CPU, memory, request rate, queue depth)?
Has capacity been tested at 1.5 times peak load via Datadog load tests or synthetic monitors?
Are P95 and P99 latency tracked and within SLO at production load?
Is a capacity model in place with growth projections?
Is performance regression testing integrated in CI?
Is predictive autoscaling in place to scale before demand arrives?
Are performance budgets enforced per release?
Is real-time performance profiling active in production?

questions.question: Are perf SLOs defined with APM regression detection on releases?
questions.industry_source: AWS WAF Performance; Google SRE; OpenTelemetry

elements.text: Is performance only monitored reactively after user complaints, with no APM regression detection in place?
Are performance SLOs defined covering p50, p95, and p99 latency?
Is Datadog APM regression detection active on each release, with rollback triggered on regression?
Is a performance regression test run in CI before merge?
Is regression history tracked and visible across releases?
Is real-time performance profiling active in production?
Are performance budgets enforced per release?
Is predictive degradation alerting in place?

questions.question: Is cloud spend tagged + monthly-reviewed with right-sizing acted on?

elements.text: Is cloud spend tagging incomplete, or are right-sizing recommendations not consistently acted on?
Is application cloud spend tagged and reviewed monthly, with right-sizing recommendations acted on?
Is a documented sustainability posture in place, covering workload placement and instance generation choices?
Is cost-per-unit tracked at the transaction or per-user level?
Is cost anomaly alerting configured?
Is reserved instance or savings-plan coverage optimised?
Are FinOps reviews conducted monthly?
Is automated cost anomaly detection and response in place?
Is real-time cost attribution available?
Do architecture decisions include cost modelling?

questions.question: Is the app stateless, with externalised config and a documented architecture pattern?

elements.text: Are there stateful processes with local files or sessions, or is configuration partly hardcoded?
Are backing services treated as anything other than attached resources?

Is the application built to 12-factor principles, with stateless processes, externalised configuration via environment variables or Parameter Store or Secrets Manager, and backing services as attached resources?
Is the architecture pattern documented (e.g. 12-factor, layered, microservices) with dependencies inventoried?
Has the architecture been reviewed within the last 12 months and is a threat model in place?
Are managed services preferred over self-operated components?
Is technical debt inventoried?
Are components isolated with a minimal blast radius?
Is the architecture continuously validated against principles using fitness functions?
Are automated compliance checks running against the architecture?
Are Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) maintained?

questions.question: Is CMDB metadata current with named owner/lead and are vendor dependencies registered?

elements.text: Is CMDB metadata partial, stale, or are third-party dependencies tracked only informally?
Is the application registered in the CMDB with current metadata, including owner, technical lead, classification, tier, and environment list?
Is a third-party and vendor dependency register maintained with named owners and contractual SLA details?
Is CMDB metadata reviewed quarterly?
Is the third-party register tracked against contractual SLAs and renewal dates?
Is CMDB metadata auto-populated from authoritative sources?
Is vendor health monitored continuously?
